product_overview: GitHub User Finder is a single-page React application that lets users search for any GitHub username and immediately view that user’s public profile details and recent public repositories. It uses the public GitHub REST API with no authentication required.
core_goals: Search for a GitHub username and retrieve the matching public profile
View key profile details such as avatar, display name, bio, location, and follower stats
Browse up to six of the user’s most recently updated public repositories
Handle empty input and not-found cases through clear user-facing messages
Inspect repository details including description, language, and star count

features.name: Username Search
features.description: Allows a user to enter a GitHub username and submit it to start a profile lookup.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> See the empty state message -> Enter a GitHub username -> Submit the search -> See the loading state -> See the user profile and repository results
Navigate to / -> See the empty state message -> Submit the search without entering a username -> See a validation alert
Navigate to / -> Enter text in the username field -> Clear the input using the clear control -> Submit the search after re-entering a username -> See the loading state -> See results

features.name: User Profile Card
features.description: Displays the fetched GitHub user’s public profile information after a successful search.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Enter a valid GitHub username -> Submit the search -> See the loading state -> See the user profile card with avatar, username link, optional name, optional bio, optional location, and public repo/follower/following stats
Navigate to / -> Enter a valid GitHub username -> Submit the search -> See the profile card with follower and following counts formatted for display

features.name: Recent Repositories
features.description: Shows up to six of the user’s most recently updated public repositories in a responsive grid.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Enter a valid GitHub username with public repositories -> Submit the search -> See the loading state -> See the Recent Repositories section with repository cards
Navigate to / -> Enter a valid GitHub username with no public repositories -> Submit the search -> See the loading state -> See the message that no public repositories were found
Navigate to / -> Enter a valid GitHub username -> Submit the search -> See repository cards with names, optional descriptions, optional language badges, and star counts

features.name: Error Handling and Retry
features.description: Shows user-facing messages when a lookup cannot be completed and lets the user reset the view to search again.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Enter a username that does not exist -> Submit the search -> See the not-found error message -> Choose to try again -> Return to the empty state

Navigate to / -> Enter input that triggers a validation issue by leaving the field empty -> Submit the search -> See the inline validation alert -> Start typing again -> See the validation message clear
Navigate to / -> Enter a username -> Submit the search -> If a lookup error is shown, choose to try again -> Return to the empty state

routes.description: Single-page GitHub User Finder — search field, profile, and repositories

features.name: GitHub user lookup
features.description: Search by GitHub username; loads profile and up to six most recently updated public repos
user_interactions: View empty state message before any search
Enter GitHub username in the username field
See validation alert when submitting without a username
Clear the input with the clear control when text is present
Observe loading UI while data loads
View user card with avatar, login link, optional name, bio, location, and repo/follower stats
View Recent Repositories section with repo cards, stars, and language when applicable
View empty repositories message when the user has no public repos
View error message when user is not found or API fails
Click Try Again to dismiss error and reset to search again

known_limitations.issue: Public GitHub REST API rate limits apply without authentication
known_limitations.impact: Repeated live API calls in automated tests may be throttled
